zh.lifeSummary: 當Ann de la Lynde於大約1466年出生在英國多瑟特文特爾布恩 可倫斯同時，她的父親Sir Thomas de la Lynd I Baron33歲，母親Edith Twynhoe29歲。她於1482年之前與Sir Thomas Trenchard在英國英格蘭多瑟特文特爾布恩 賀林斯同結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1569年在英國英格蘭哈普爾沃爾維頓享年104歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: la
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Lynde
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Lind
meanings: Scandinavian, English, and Dutch: topographic name for someone who lived by a lime tree, Scandinavian and Middle English lind. As a Swedish name, it is often of ornamental origin.
North German and Danish: habitational name from any of various places called with Lind ‘lime tree’.
South German, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): nickname for a gentle, mild, tolerant man, from Middle High German lint, linde, Yiddish lind ‘gentle, mild’.

nameMeanings.name: Ann
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Variant spelling of Anne . In the U.S. Ann is the more frequent spelling.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

en.lifeSummary: When Ann de la Lynde was born about 1466, in Winterbourne Clenstone, Dorset, England, her father, Sir Thomas de la Lynd I Baron, was 33 and her mother, Edith Twynhoe, was 29. She married Sir Thomas Trenchard before 1482, in Winterbourne Herringstone, Dorset, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 2 daughters. She died in 1569, in Wolverton, Hampshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 104.

Elle a épousé Sir Thomas Trenchard avant 1482, à Winterbourne Herringstone, Dorset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée en 1569, à Wolverton, Hampshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 104 ans.

Sie hat vor 1482 in Winterbourne Herringstone, Dorset, England, United Kingdom Sir Thomas Trenchard geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb 1569 im Alter von 104 Jahren in Wolverton, Hampshire, England, United Kingdom.

ja.lifeSummary: Ann de la Lyndeが約1466年にイギリスドールセトウィンテルボウルン クレンストーンで生まれた時，父Sir Thomas de la Lynd I Baronは33歳，母Edith Twynhoeは29歳であった。1482年以前にイギリスイギリスドールセトウィンテルボウルン ハーリングストーンでSir Thomas Trenchardと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1569年にイギリスイギリスハンプシュアイアウオルバートンで104歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Ann de la Lynde(이)가 대략 1466년 영국 도르세트 윈터보우른 클렌스톤에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Thomas de la Lynd I Baron(은)는 33세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Edith Twynhoe(은)는 29세였다. 그녀는 1482년 이전 영국 잉글랜드 도르세트 윈터보우른 허링스톤에서 Sir Thomas Trenchard(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1569년 영국 잉글랜드 함프실르 울버턴에서 104세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Ann de la Lynde родилась около 1466 г. в местности винтирбурн кленстоун, дорсет, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir Thomas de la Lynd I Baron было 33 года, а матери, которую звали Edith Twynhoe, 29. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Thomas Trenchard перед 1482 г. в местности винтирбурн хиррингстоун, дорсет, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла в 1569 г. в местности волвиртон, хампшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 104 лет.

Se casó con Sir Thomas Trenchard antes de 1482, en Winterbourne Herringstone, Dorset, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 2 hijas.
